conditional_states.condition: Modal confirms a completed action before or as it closes.
Modal renders content that may be absent (e.g. a picker with no items).
Primary modal action is gated until inputs are valid.
Modal edits a record that changed underneath it before confirm.
Modal becomes a full-height sheet with 44px targets on coarse-pointer devices.
Open/close transition must degrade to an instant show/hide.

rationale: A modal seizes the user's focus, so correct focus management is the defining requirement: focus moves into the dialog on open, is trapped within it, returns to the trigger on close, and Escape dismisses. Because modals usually host an async confirm, in-dialog loading and error states are required so the user is not bounced out on failure.
evidence: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices dialog pattern: initial focus, focus trap, focus restore, Escape to close.
WCAG 2.2 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.1.2 (No Keyboard Trap, with intentional dialog exception via Escape).
NN/g: keep validation and error feedback inside the modal rather than dismissing it on failure.
